description:
You are a build-engineer who needs a quick metric from yesterday’s continuous-integration run.

    1. A single plain-text log file already exists at  
       /home/user/builds/jenkins_build.log  

       • The file is UTF-8 encoded.  
       • A successful build is always recorded as the literal string  
         BUILD SUCCESS (all capitals, one space, no punctuation).  
       • Your goal is to count how many times that exact string appears anywhere
         in the log.

    2. Write the result to a new file called  
       /home/user/builds/analysis/success_count.log  
       (create the analysis directory if it is missing).

    3. Output format – this is what the automated tests will validate:  

          SUCCESS_COUNT=<number>  

       • The word SUCCESS_COUNT must be in all capitals.  
       • The equal sign = must have no surrounding spaces.  
       • <number> must be the exact count of the matches.  
       • End the line with a single UNIX newline (LF).  
       • No additional text, spaces, blank lines, or trailing
         whitespace are allowed.

    4. Accomplish everything with just one terminal command (pipeline
       counts as one command).  Do not open an editor; the task is meant
       to be solvable entirely from the shell.

    When finished, the file /home/user/builds/analysis/success_count.log
    should exist in the exact format shown above and contain the correct
    count.  That file is the only artifact the grader will inspect.
truth:
Initial file that is already present before the agent begins:

    Path: /home/user/builds/jenkins_build.log  
    Contents (UTF-8, LF line endings):

    [2023-09-01 12:00:00] Starting build
    [2023-09-01 12:05:00] BUILD SUCCESS
    [2023-09-02 14:10:17] Starting build
    [2023-09-02 14:15:30] BUILD FAILURE
    [2023-09-03 09:00:00] Starting build
    [2023-09-03 09:07:42] BUILD SUCCESS
    [2023-09-04 11:22:10] Starting build
    [2023-09-04 11:29:58] BUILD SUCCESS

    (Exactly three occurrences of the string “BUILD SUCCESS”.)

    Directory that exists beforehand:  
    /home/user/builds

    Directory that is NOT present beforehand and must be created by the
    agent:  
    /home/user/builds/analysis

    Expected file after agent completes:

    Path: /home/user/builds/analysis/success_count.log  
    Permissions: 0644 (agent default is fine)  
    Contents (single line ending with LF):

    SUCCESS_COUNT=3
name: task_000000_693ebaf5
